steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Using $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ with $a = 1$, $b = -2$, $c = -18$:
$$x = \frac{2 \pm \sqrt{4 + 72}}{2} = \frac{2 \pm \sqrt{76}}{2}$$
Simplify by separating the fraction:
$$x = 1 \pm \frac{\sqrt{76}}{2}$$
Since $\sqrt{76} = \sqrt{4 \cdot 19} = 2\sqrt{19}$, we have:
$$x = 1 \pm \frac{2\sqrt{19}}{2} = 1 \pm \sqrt{19}$$

steps.title: Match to the given form
steps.content: The positive solution is $x = 1 + \sqrt{19}$. Comparing to the form $1 + \sqrt{k}$, we see that $k = 19$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
